opp.name: Nissan_S2P_2026
opp.next_step: Confidence 10% 3rd of July more than 6 months the customer in silent mode , email outreach for meeting intro and to evaluate this opportunity 6th April - Have paused the project due to the war, but the ceasefire is giving confidence to restart in early-mid May when all will be back to the office - current situation is that the EMEA HQ 'may' insist on a solution used elsewhere, which could be us (US & Australia) or alternative(s) (not clear who) - next step is to share a process flow that we will need to run a live custom demo against, also interested in getting a high-level ROI analysis (not a full Value Assessment) - intention is for DQ to spend some time with project lead and CPO to build this out at the face-to-face demo session in May. 28th Jan - Provided a high-level budgetary estimate only heavily caveated - likely to be lower in scale in reality as only a small team, have nudged again to get the demo back on track before Ramadan/QBR trip. 20th Jan - Responded that its early stages, don't have the data to complete the tables, but want a high level estimate, suggest call to discuss and confirm demo dates. 9th Jan - Interested in a budgetary estimate, shared the estimation tables and requested a call/meeting to run through 8th Jan - Discovery call held with Vinoth (HOP) and two colleagues - positively received (only gap is market analysis - they are using Beroe in other regions, we will need to flesh out a story around our Smart Cube 'integration') - next step is a live demo, Dan & Tanya to propose a structure, Salonee to provide date options asap. 18th Dec - Discovery call postponed to new year - 8th Jan. 17th Dec - Inbound lead requesting a demo on Tues 23rd Dec

items.quote: the EMEA HQ 'may' insist on a solution used elsewhere, which could be us (US & Australia) or alternative(s) (not clear who)
only gap is market analysis - they are using Beroe in other regions, we will need to flesh out a story around our Smart Cube 'integration'

items.source: OpportunityContactRole (only 2 buyer-side roles logged against an 8-person account contact list)

metrics.narrative: Vinoth Kumar articulated clear qualitative pain and requirements on the 7 Jan 2026 discovery call ("full end-to-end automation of PR to invoice processes, enhanced tail-spend visibility, AI-driven sourcing capabilities, and integrated market intelligence") but no dollar figure, headcount, or cycle-time number was quantified. The buyer separately asked for a 'high-level ROI analysis (not a full Value Assessment)' per Next_Step__c (6 Apr 2026), showing intent to build a business case, but that analysis was never confirmed delivered.

champion.narrative: Vinoth Kumar developed real champion signal on the 7 Jan 2026 call: he initiated outreach, attended with colleagues, articulated requirements clearly, arranged to loop in his Australian colleagues to learn about Zycus's existing use there, and is nominally driving toward an RFQ. Salonee Karnik was tasked with coordinating the demo and managing the RFP process operationally. What is missing: neither has been heard from since the 7 Jan 2026 call other than the 6-month-later renewed outreach on 3 Jul 2026, which has not yet been answered, so current engagement reads as cooling/at risk rather than active.

competition.narrative: SAP Ariba is logged as a named competitor in Competitors__c, but no call or note shows Nissan actively evaluating it; the current stack being displaced is SAP plus eBa plus manual email/spreadsheets. The one concrete, unresolved competitive gap is market intelligence: the buyer uses Beroe in other regions and Zycus has no native equivalent, flagged as the 'only gap' on the 8 Jan 2026 discovery call and still open. The wedge is building a credible Smart Cube integration story before any renewed demo.

identify_pain.narrative: The buyer described current SAP and eBa systems plus manual emails and spreadsheets as 'hindering indirect procurement across the Middle East, Africa, Turkey, Egypt, and future India markets' on the 7 Jan 2026 call. MEDDPICC 2.0 corroborates: no RFQ capability in the current eBa tool, heavy reliance on Outlook for all procurement, and manual monthly market-intelligence research by the EU team. Pain owner is the procurement function under Vinoth Kumar and Salonee Karnik.

paper_process.narrative: No contracting mechanics (MSA, SOW, legal review, procurement channel) have surfaced in any source, which is expected at this early Qualified/pre-RFQ stage. Visibility would come from getting the RFQ formally launched, since Salonee Karnik is slated to own that process once it starts.

economic_buyer.narrative: MEDDPICC 2.0 (updated 2026-06-04) states no economic buyer is explicitly identified: Vinoth Kumar heads procurement for the Middle East/Europe region and is the main driver, and intends to invite his 'process team' globally for the demo, suggesting broader stakeholder sign-off is required. Vinoth stated on the 7 Jan 2026 call that budget has been secured but a target price would only be shared after Zycus's proposal. Next_Step__c (6 Apr 2026) adds real uncertainty: regional headquarters overseeing this account may mandate a platform already used elsewhere, meaning the true final decision authority may sit outside the local team entirely.

decision_process.narrative: MEDDPICC 2.0 maps the process as pre-RFQ discovery, internal stakeholder alignment, a formal RFQ, evaluation, then vendor selection; Vinoth Kumar described the 7 Jan 2026 call itself as a 'get-to-know' session ahead of the official RFQ, with Salonee Karnik tasked to 'coordinate and schedule the detailed demo with the ME and Europe process teams and manage the RFP process moving forward.' That process has not advanced past this discovery step in the six months since: no RFQ has launched and no demo has been scheduled.

decision_criteria.narrative: Both MEDDPICC 2.0 and the 7 Jan 2026 discovery call converge on the same criteria: end-to-end automation of the source-to-pay process, AI/market-intelligence integration as a key differentiator ask, replacing Outlook-based manual workflows, SAP HANA integration, and a supplier portal for invoice submission. On the call, the buyer directly asked, 'Does the supplier have a portal where they go and upload their invoice against each purchase order?' and, 'Is it going to be a SaaS model or how does you guys operate?' confirming these are live evaluation criteria, not assumptions.

items.name: New Zycus POC introduction & restart request
items.summary: Mohamad Alhakim (Zycus RVP Middle East) emails Vinoth and Salonee to introduce himself as the new primary contact replacing Dan Quinn, referencing the earlier-year discussions on sourcing/contracting/invoicing priorities across ME, Africa, Turkey and Egypt, and proposes a 20-minute call the week of July 13 to reassess where things stand — reply pending from Nissan.

day_summary.overall: After months of dormancy since the January demo scheduling thread stalled, Zycus re-engaged with a new regional VP, Mohamad Alhakim, taking over as primary contact after Dan Quinn's departure. Alhakim's email to Vinoth and Salonee is essentially a relationship-continuity play — signaling he's briefed on the ME/Africa/Turkey/Egypt procurement transformation agenda and asking to restart the conversation with a 20-minute call the week of July 13. The ball is now in Nissan's court to confirm availability; nothing substantive on scope or commercials has moved yet.

cro_panel.intro: This partner/HQ-adjacent, APAC-region S2P deal received a strong, well-attended discovery call on 7 Jan 2026 (~6 months ago) that surfaced clear pain and detailed decision criteria, but has since gone quiet; no demo, no RFQ launch, and no confirmed buyer contact since roughly 20 Jan 2026, despite a war-related pause and a missed May 2026 restart target.; The deal owner's own 3 Jul 2026 next-step log states the customer has been in 'silent mode' for more than six months; a fresh re-engagement email was sent that day, with no reply logged as of this sweep (6 days later)

cro_panel.header: Nissan Africa, Middle East & India — Qualified · $200K · closes 31 Jul

contributions.detail: 183 days of buyer silence despite active rep chasing; the war-related pause was never converted into a restart after the April ceasefire.
No economic buyer on record; EMEA HQ could mandate an alternate/unnamed platform, a decision Zycus has no visibility into or relationship to shape.
Only 53 days to the 31 Aug 2026 close date with the deal essentially inactive — high odds of further slippage or stage inflation vs. reality.
Status-quo/incumbent SAP-eBa baseline plus an unresolved Beroe market-intelligence gap leave Zycus exposed on both inertia and a stated requirement.

win_position.text: Deal has sat in Qualified for ~181 days with the one planned next step — a live demo — never delivered despite being teed up since January.
No economic buyer identified; EMEA HQ may mandate a platform used elsewhere in the region, and Zycus has no relationship at that level to influence it.
Buyer has gone dark 183 days; rep's own July note pegs confidence at 10%, and only rough, heavily-caveated budget numbers have been exchanged — no paper process started.
Buyer's Beroe market-intelligence gap, raised on the 8 Jan call, remains unanswered six months later.
Champion signal exists but is stale: Vinoth Kumar drove the original demo request and a positive discovery call — needs reconfirmation, not assumed.

No scheduled future meeting, no logged next-step history, no dated forward milestone — nothing on the calendar.

Earlier cycle showed real pull: Vinoth Kumar (HOP) initiated the original inbound demo request and the 8 Jan discovery call was 'positively received.'

fit.expectations_fit: Fit read is based entirely on the 7 Jan 2026 discovery call (background context, not reconfirmed in the six months since); presented here as the buyer's stated criteria against Zycus's scope.
fit.positioning_strength: AI/market intelligence was named a key differentiator ask by the buyer, but the specific gap versus Beroe (used elsewhere by the buyer) remains unanswered six months after being raised, leaving Zycus under-positioned on this specific point

confidence_signals.summary: A strong opening call is now six months stale with only a just-sent, unanswered re-engagement email as the newest signal.

north_star_verdict.math: Created 2026-01-07, 181 days in Qualified as of 7 Jul 2026 with no stage movement. Close date 2026-08-31 is 55 days out. Last content-rich buyer touch was the 7 Jan 2026 discovery call; the last genuine two-way buyer contact (an inbound email reply) was 2026-01-13 (175 days ago). The only newer activity is a rep-initiated re-engagement email on 2026-07-03 (4 days ago), still awaiting reply. No demo, RFQ, or economic-buyer conversation has happened. Reaching a decision in the remaining 55 days from Qualified with none of these steps underway is not realistic.
evidence: Next_Step__c (3 Jul 2026): 'more than 6 months the customer in silent mode, email outreach for meeting intro and to evaluate this opportunity'
Next_Step__c (6 Apr 2026): project paused due to war, restart targeted early-mid May 2026, which did not occur
Avoma discovery call notes, 2026-01-07: demo agenda and ROI build session were planned but never scheduled
north_star_verdict.headline: A promising discovery call on 7 Jan 2026 stalled for six months when the regional conflict paused the project; a re-engagement email went out 3 Jul 2026 but the buyer has not yet replied, and the 31 Aug 2026 close date is not achievable on current pace.
north_star_verdict.recommended_forecast: Omitted / push close date - no EB confirmed, no demo held, no RFQ launched, and buyer has been silent for 6 months

best_practice_check.summary: Three deal-specific levers stand out given the stall and the thin thread count.

competitive_position.summary: SAP Ariba sits in Salesforce as a named competitor but has no supporting call or quote evidence of active engagement in this evaluation. The real, currently live competitive issue is the market-intelligence gap versus Beroe, flagged as the buyer's only objection on the 8 Jan 2026 discovery call and still unresolved six months later. The current SAP plus eBa manual stack is the do-nothing baseline being displaced.

competitors.name: SAP Ariba
competitors.how_we_win: No direct evaluation evidence found; keep monitoring since it is logged as a named competitor, but lead with the existing Nissan Australia/US relationship and decision-criteria fit instead of a head-to-head comparison.

competitors.name: Beroe (market analysis / category intelligence)
competitors.quote: they are using Beroe in other regions, we will need to flesh out a story around our integration
competitors.how_we_win: Build a category-intelligence integration narrative (partnership, data feed, or native capability roadmap); demonstrate how Zycus + market data delivers unified S2P+insights vs point-solution stitching; include this in the demo when restarted.

competitors.name: SAP / eBa (current stack, do-nothing baseline)
competitors.quote: current SAP and eBa systems plus manual emails and spreadsheets are hindering indirect procurement across the Middle East, Africa, Turkey, Egypt, and future India markets
competitors.how_we_win: Quantify the cost of the manual/Outlook-based process and the eBa RFQ gap to make the automation case concrete.

competitors.name: Beroe (market-intelligence gap)
competitors.quote: only gap is market analysis - they are using Beroe in other regions, we will need to flesh out a story around our Smart Cube 'integration'
competitors.how_we_win: Deliver a concrete Smart Cube integration story before any renewed demo; this was Zycus's own committed action item from the 7 Jan 2026 call and remains outstanding.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: This partner/HQ-adjacent, APAC-region S2P deal received a strong, well-attended discovery call on 7 Jan 2026 (~6 months ago) that surfaced clear pain and detailed decision criteria, but has since gone quiet: no demo, no RFQ launch, and no confirmed buyer contact since roughly 20 Jan 2026, despite a war-related pause and a missed May 2026 restart target. The deal owner's own 3 Jul 2026 next-step log states the customer has been in 'silent mode' for more than six months; a fresh re-engagement email was sent that day, with no reply logged as of this sweep (6 days later).

deal_risk.text: Nissan's EMEA headquarters may mandate a platform used elsewhere in the group, and Zycus has no confirmed relationship at that headquarters level, leaving vendor-selection authority unclear.
The market-intelligence gap versus Beroe (used by the buyer in other regions), flagged on 7 Jan 2026, remains unresolved six months later.

Only two buyer-side contact roles are recorded on this opportunity (Ziad Baroud, Salonee Karnik), with Vinoth Kumar carried as a partner-adjacent contact -- a thin, effectively single-threaded relationship for a deal spanning Middle East, Africa, Turkey, Egypt and future India markets.

win_position.text: The 7 Jan 2026 discovery call with Vinoth Kumar (HOP) and colleagues was well-attended and positively received, surfacing detailed pain (manual SAP/eBa/Outlook processes, no RFQ tool, poor spend visibility) and five concrete decision criteria.

No economic buyer is confirmed: MEDDPICC 2.0 states the EB is 'not explicitly identified,' and the deal carries a structural risk that Nissan's EMEA headquarters may mandate a platform used elsewhere, a decision outside the local team's authority.

The opportunity has sat in Qualified stage for roughly six months (created 7 Jan 2026) with no demo, no RFQ launch, and no confirmed buyer contact since around 20 Jan 2026 -- well past the ~30-day cadence expected at this stage.

Zycus already runs live at Nissan in Australia (since 2018/2021) and the US per MEDDPICC notes, giving a relationship foothold if EMEA HQ does standardize on a platform already used elsewhere in the group.
The face-to-face demo and ROI workshop targeted for early-to-mid May 2026 (post-ceasefire) never took place, and no replacement date has been set as of this sweep (9 Jul 2026).

deal_momentum.text: The deal owner sent a re-engagement email on 3 Jul 2026 (6 days ago) after logging that the customer has been in silent mode for more than six months; no buyer reply is on record yet.
No completed buyer meeting since the 7 Jan 2026 discovery call; the only other Avoma-tracked touchpoint (23 Dec 2025) was not recorded.
A genuine two-way buyer email exchange is confirmed as recently as 13 Jan 2026, showing the relationship was live and responsive early in the cycle.
A war-related pause (noted 6 Apr 2026) and a missed May 2026 restart window have left the deal without forward motion for roughly six months.

customer_commitment.text: Buyer commitments made on the 7 Jan 2026 call (share the internal S2P process flow, share budget targets after reviewing the proposal, confirm demo time slots) remain unconfirmed roughly six months later.
Vinoth Kumar personally initiated the original inbound request for a demo (17 Dec 2025) and actively engaged throughout the 7 Jan 2026 discovery call, indicating a real internal driver at the time.

customer_expectations_fit.summary: Core process-automation and invoicing criteria line up well with the current product scope; the market-intelligence and ERP-integration criteria remain unconfirmed.

hard.ais_why:
• On Jan 7 2026, Vinoth Kumar (Nissan ME, Head of Procurement) said in the discovery call (note from Dan Quinn): 'Key requirements include full end-to-end automation of PR to invoice processes, enhanced tail-spend visibility, AI-driven sourcing capabilities, and integrated market intelligence.' He continued: 'the system will give me an indication that based on the market intelligence report, you are now capable to run an RFQ for this particular sourcing so that you can benefit out of the new technology or the cost.' Buyer explicitly naming AI-driven sourcing as a mandatory requirement.
• Per the 6 Apr 2026 rep note: 'Have paused the project due to the war... EMEA HQ may insist on a solution used elsewhere, which could be us (US & Australia) or alternative(s)... next step is to share a process flow that we will need to run a live custom demo against, also interested in getting a high-level ROI analysis.' Vinoth wants a live custom demo built on his own process flow - signaling deep solution-led evaluation.
• High confidence: 2 valid Avoma meetings (nissan-me.ae domain), Jan 7 transcript with 114 notes including direct Vinoth quotes naming AI-driven sourcing and market intelligence integration. Product scope includes Merlin + Merlin Intake + Agentic AI + ANA. Project is paused due to war but the AI vision was articulated by the buyer pre-pause - AI Hungry stands.
hard.next_step:
Confidence 10%

3rd of July more than 6 months the customer in silent mode , email outreach for meeting intro and to evaluate this opportunity

6th April - Have paused the project due to the war, but the ceasefire is giving confidence to restart in early-mid May when all will be back to the office - current situation is that the EMEA HQ 'may' insist on a solution used elsewhere, which could be us (US & Australia) or alternative(s) (not clear who) - next step is to share a process flow that we will need to run a live custom demo against, also interested in getting a high-level ROI analysis (not a full Value Assessment) - intention is for DQ to spend some time with project lead and CPO to build this out at the face-to-face demo session in May.

28th Jan - Provided a high-level budgetary estimate only heavily caveated - likely to be lower in scale in reality as only a small team, have nudged again to get the demo back on track before Ramadan/QBR trip.

20th Jan - Responded that its early stages, don't have the data to complete the tables, but want a high level estimate, suggest call to discuss and confirm demo dates.

9th Jan - Interested in a budgetary estimate, shared the estimation tables and requested a call/meeting to run through

8th Jan - Discovery call held with Vinoth (HOP) and two colleagues - positively received (only gap is market analysis - they are using Beroe in other regions, we will need to flesh out a story around our Smart Cube 'integration') - next step is a live demo, Dan & Tanya to propose a structure, Salonee to provide date options asap.

18th Dec - Discovery call postponed to new year - 8th Jan.

17th Dec - Inbound lead requesting a demo on Tues 23rd Dec

business_case.evidence: No ROI or savings quantification has been confirmed. The buyer requested a 'high-level ROI analysis' per Next_Step__c (6 Apr 2026), but that request itself is outside the 30-60 day recency window and was never confirmed delivered.

gaps: Named identity of the alternative platform(s) regional headquarters might mandate is unknown (Next_Step__c, 6 Apr 2026, flags the risk but does not name a vendor) - a genuine knowledge gap, not a fabricated competitor.
AIS_Score__c / AIS_Status__c / AIS_Why__c were not available in this sweep's data pull; the AI-fit read relies only on Products__c and the buyer's stated AI/market-intelligence interest.
Only 1 of 2 discovered Avoma touchpoints was transcribed/read; the 23 Dec 2025 discovery call has no recording (not_recorded) and its content is unknown.
Ziad Baroud (Evaluator, contact role) has no recorded call attendance or task activity in the evidence read; his level of engagement is unconfirmed.
Vinoth Kumar's Opportunity Contact Role is tagged as partner/third-party despite call evidence showing him acting as the internal buyer-side champion/driver - an unresolved hygiene gap.

customer_preference.evidence: No buyer call or communication content is available within the last 30-60 days; the last content-rich buyer contact was the 7 Jan 2026 discovery call, well outside this window, and the only newer event (3 Jul 2026) is a rep-initiated email with no buyer reply yet.
